orientation: All effectAlleleFwd values and genotypeInterpretation keys are on the forward (+) GRCh37 strand, to compare directly against 23andMe / AncestryDNA / MyHeritage genotypes. Genotype keys are alphabetically sorted (e.g. 'AG', not 'GA').

genotypeInterpretation.AA: Met/Met (158Met). Lower COMT enzyme activity; associated in some studies with the so-called 'worrier' profile (more dopamine in prefrontal cortex). Effect on real-world behaviour is small.
genotypeInterpretation.GG: Val/Val (158Val). Higher COMT activity ('warrior' profile in some studies). Small effect.

entries.caveats: COMT 'warrior/worrier' framing is widely overstated. Effects on cognition and stress are small and context-dependent.

genotypeInterpretation.CC: *1F/*1F — 'slow' caffeine metabolizer; some studies link higher intake to cardiovascular risk in slow metabolizers.

entries.caveats: Caffeine response is also driven by smoking, medications, and habitual intake. Limited, sometimes conflicting evidence.

genotypeInterpretation.AA: 677TT — reduced MTHFR enzyme activity (~30% of normal); may modestly raise homocysteine, especially with low folate.

entries.caveats: MTHFR testing is not recommended for most people by major genetics bodies (ACMG). Adequate dietary folate generally offsets the effect.
Interpreted alongside rs1801133. Clinical relevance for the general population is limited.

genotypeInterpretation.TT: Met/Met (66Met) — less common; studied in relation to memory and stress response, small effects.

entries.caveats: Associations with cognition and mood are modest and inconsistent across populations.

genotypeInterpretation.TT: A1/A1 (Taq1A) — associated with lower striatal D2 receptor density in some studies.

entries.caveats: Reward/addiction associations are weak and frequently fail to replicate.

APOE genotype requires BOTH rs429358 and rs7412. If rs429358 is absent from your file, ε4 status is indeterminate. APOE ε4 raises Alzheimer's risk but is not deterministic. Confirm with a clinical-grade test and discuss with a clinician before acting.
APOE ε-genotype is the combination of rs429358 and rs7412. Interpret together, never in isolation. Educational only — not a diagnosis.

genotypeInterpretation.GG: No Factor V Leiden allele detected at this position.
genotypeInterpretation.AG: Heterozygous Factor V Leiden carrier — increased venous thrombosis risk.
genotypeInterpretation.AA: Homozygous Factor V Leiden — substantially increased venous thrombosis risk.

entries.caveats: Some arrays report this SNP on the opposite strand (C/T). Confirm any positive with a clinical-grade test before any decision. Risk depends on heterozygous vs homozygous status and other factors.

genotypeInterpretation.AA: C282Y homozygous — main genotype associated with hereditary haemochromatosis (iron overload). Penetrance is incomplete.

entries.caveats: Many C282Y homozygotes never develop clinical iron overload. Confirm with iron studies and a clinical-grade test; discuss with a clinician.

genotypeInterpretation.CC: RR — produces alpha-actinin-3; over-represented among power/sprint athletes.
genotypeInterpretation.TT: XX — no functional alpha-actinin-3; more common in endurance contexts.

entries.caveats: A nudge, not a verdict. Training type, consistency, and recovery dominate athletic outcomes far more than this genotype.

genotypeInterpretation.AA: Ser/Ser (482Ser) — studied in relation to endurance traits, weak effects.

entries.caveats: Limited and inconsistent evidence. Training dominates.

genotypeInterpretation.AA: Two copies of the FTO 'A' allele — associated with a small average increase in BMI and appetite.
genotypeInterpretation.AT: One copy of the 'A' allele — smaller average effect.

entries.caveats: Real but small effect (roughly a kilogram or so on average per allele in populations). Energy balance, diet quality, sleep, and activity dominate. Not an excuse or a guarantee.

genotypeInterpretation.CC: Two copies near MC4R associated with a small average increase in BMI and appetite.
genotypeInterpretation.TT: No risk allele at this position.

entries.caveats: Small effect on average body weight. Lifestyle factors dominate. You may find appetite regulation slightly harder, not destiny.

genotypeInterpretation.CC: Two copies of the myopia-associated allele near GJD2.
genotypeInterpretation.CT: One copy of the myopia-associated allele.
genotypeInterpretation.TT: No myopia-associated allele at this position.

entries.caveats: Common-variant myopia risk is highly polygenic; any single SNP explains very little. More daylight/outdoor time in childhood is a protective factor against myopia progression.

genotypeInterpretation.CC: Y402H homozygous — higher associated risk of age-related macular degeneration (AMD).
genotypeInterpretation.CT: Y402H heterozygous — intermediate associated AMD risk.
genotypeInterpretation.TT: No Y402H risk allele at this position.

entries.caveats: There is no genetic intervention that improves eyesight. For AMD risk, not smoking, a healthy diet, and discussing AREDS-type supplement guidance with an ophthalmologist are the evidence-based levers.

genotypeInterpretation.TT: A69S homozygous — higher associated risk of age-related macular degeneration (AMD).
genotypeInterpretation.GT: A69S heterozygous — intermediate associated AMD risk.
genotypeInterpretation.GG: No A69S risk allele at this position.

entries.caveats: AMD risk is modifiable by lifestyle (especially not smoking). This reports risk only — it does not promise any vision improvement. Discuss screening with an ophthalmologist.
